title: 8th Pay Commission: NC-JCM Staff Side seeks ₹69,000 minimum pay, 6% increment annually – check details
summary: The National Council-JCM proposes a minimum basic pay of ₹69,000 for the 8th Pay Commission, aiming to benefit over 50 lakh government employees. Recommendations include higher increments, pay restructuring, and HRA indexing to inflation for better financial support amid rising costs.

title: The tax regime debate is back, and your salary for this year just got dragged in
summary: Income Tax Rules 2026 have made the old regime more attractive by raising exemption limits. For FY27, salaried individuals must recalculate before choosing between old and new.

summary: PPF, a popular investment in India, offers tax benefits and stable interest rates for conservative investors. Here's how much you need to invest each year to build a ₹1 crore corpus and how long it takes.&nbsp;

title: Gold loan portfolio grows 3.8x, outstandings surpasses personal loans in December 2025 — What we know
summary: As of December 2025, outstanding gold loan borrowings surpassed personal loans, totaling ₹16.8 lakh crore from 4.7 crore borrowers, as per a TransUnion CIBIL report. It also noted increasing ticket sizes and borrower exposure.

title: RBI moots 1-hour pause on high-value UPI payments to curb fraud: Experts warn of potential friction, delayed settlements
summary: The RBI’s proposed one-hour payment pause on select transactions aims to curb digital fraud. However, experts highlight potential friction, delayed settlements and challenges to a seamless payment experience.

title: What US green card holders should know about exit tax and gifting rules
summary: Under US tax law, individuals who give up citizenship or relinquish a long-term green card (held for eight years or more) may be subject to the exit tax provisions under the internal revenue code

title: Can both parents invest ₹3 lakh in child’s PPF account? PPF limit, guardian rules and tax compliance explained
summary: Confusion around PPF investment rules often leads to the misconception that both parents can contribute ₹3 lakh to a child’s PPF account, however the annual PPF investment limit is capped at ₹1.5 lakh per individual account as per regulatory guidelines, regardless of parental contributions.
